summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Chi-square test for homogeneity'?

"Chi-square test for homogeneity" means: Tests H₀: the distribution of a categorical variable is the same across two or more populations. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Homogeneity vs. independence'?

"Homogeneity vs. independence" means: Same χ² calculation, but homogeneity compares distributions across known groups; independence tests association within one population. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Study design difference'?

"Study design difference" means: Independence: one random sample classified by two variables; Homogeneity: separate random samples from each population. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Follow-up analysis'?

"Follow-up analysis" means: After rejecting H₀, compare individual cell contributions to χ² or construct pairwise comparisons to identify which groups differ. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Expected counts'?

In Chi-Square Test for Homogeneity, "Expected counts" refers to: Calculated the same way as the independence test: E = (row total × column total) / grand total.
